title: waltheria microphylla
intro: Waltheria microphylla, also known as the West Indian nettle or bitter leaf, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that offer several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Waltheria microphylla is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to alleviate various health issues.
